summary: Compliance posture is moderate. The codebase is well-structured with explicit error handling, clear access boundaries, and no hardcoded secrets detected. However, lack of audit logging, no explicit encryption layer, and minimal enforcement of code review gates are concerns. Plugin system has load order validation but no capability restrictions.

recommendations.title: Implement audit logging for file operations and plugin system
recommendations.description: Currently no audit trail exists for file writes, plugin loads, or command executions. Add immutable audit log (append-only file in ~/.config/alfred/audit.log) recording who (session ID) did what when.

recommendations.title: Add plugin capability restrictions (sandboxing)
recommendations.description: Plugins currently have unrestricted access to all bridge primitives. Implement a capability model restricting plugins to declared capabilities (e.g., 'filesystem', 'keybinding', 'ui'). HIGH: plugins can call (save-buffer) without user consent.

recommendations.title: Enforce pre-commit hooks for code quality gates
recommendations.description: CI runs checks but pre-commit hooks are not enforced. Add .pre-commit-config.yaml requiring format, lint, and test checks before commit.

recommendations.title: Add encryption for sensitive files at rest
recommendations.description: User config files (~/.config/alfred/*) and buffer snapshots are stored unencrypted. Consider optional encryption of clipboard content and buffer history.

recommendations.title: Document security model and threat assumptions
recommendations.description: No SECURITY.md exists. Document threat model: assumes trusted user (local machine), no multi-user isolation, plugins are trusted code.
